evals.prompt: Tag my most recent trail run
evals.expected_output: A classification card with Purpose, Modality (Trail running), and Time-of-day tag. Purpose should reflect distance/duration/elevation relative to Tommi's thresholds. Hill modifier should appear if elevation > 40 m/km.
expectations: Output contains a PURPOSE field
Output contains a MODALITY field with 'Trail running'
Output contains a TIME field (Morning/Daytime/Evening/Night)
Output includes a 'Why' explanation referencing at least one concrete data point (distance, duration, elevation, or HR zones)
Hill modifier is correctly applied or omitted based on elevation per km calculation (threshold 40 m/km)

evals.prompt: Classify my most recent swimming session
evals.expected_output: Classification card with Modality = Swimming, Purpose = Cross-training (or Recovery if short/easy), Time-of-day derived from start time.
expectations: Output contains a PURPOSE field
Output contains a MODALITY field with 'Swimming'
Output contains a TIME field
Purpose is Cross-training or Recovery (not Aerobic base or Interval unless HR/label strongly indicates otherwise)
No Hill modifier applied (swimming has no elevation)

evals.prompt: What kind of session was my longest run in February 2026?
evals.expected_output: Should find the ~21km Vega de San Mateo trail run from Feb 19. Classification should be Very long / Race sim (duration 3h45m exceeds 3h threshold), Modality = Trail running, with Hill modifier (62.7 m/km exceeds 40 m/km threshold). Time = Morning or Daytime based on 11:06 start.
expectations: Correctly identifies the February 2026 longest run (approximately 21 km)
PURPOSE is Long effort or Very long / Race sim (duration 3h45m is the key signal)
MODALITY is Trail running
TIME is Morning or Daytime (activity started around 11:00)
Output includes distance and elevation figures from the actual activity data

evals.prompt: Tag my Helsinki trail run from November 23, 2025
evals.expected_output: Helsinki Polkujuoksu from Nov 23 2025. 7.22km, 526m elevation gain, 72.9 m/km. Aerobic base (62min under 90min threshold, 7.2km under 15km). Hill modifier fires (72.9 > 40 m/km). Technical terrain should NOT fire: actual pace ~11.1 min/km is fully explained by the elevation (expected ~11.1 min/km with 6.75 baseline + 72.9 x 0.06 penalty), ratio ~1.00. Morning session (start 09:52).
expectations: PURPOSE is Aerobic base (distance 7.2 km and duration ~80 min are both below Long effort thresholds)
Hill modifier is applied (elevation 72.9 m/km exceeds 40 m/km threshold)
Technical terrain modifier is NOT applied (pace is fully explained by the elevation gain — ratio close to 1.0)
MODALITY is Trail running
TIME is Daytime (activity started at 09:52, which is in the 09:00-16:59 Daytime window)

evals.prompt: Tag my Helsinki trail run from January 10, 2026
evals.expected_output: Helsinki Polkujuoksu from Jan 10 2026. 10.9km, 256m, 23.5 m/km, duration 114 min. Long effort (duration > 90 min). Hill modifier does NOT fire (23.5 < 40 m/km). Technical terrain: must use Long effort baseline (7.5 min/km), expected = 7.5 + (23.5 x 0.06) = 8.91 min/km, actual ~10.5 min/km, ratio ~1.18 — below 1.25 threshold, so technical terrain does NOT fire. A naive model using Aerobic base baseline (6.75) would incorrectly flag this as technical (ratio 1.28). Evening session (start 15:17).
expectations: PURPOSE is Long effort (duration 114 min exceeds 90 min threshold)
Hill modifier is NOT applied (elevation 23.5 m/km is below 40 m/km threshold)
Technical terrain modifier is NOT applied (correct Long effort baseline of 7.5 min/km gives ratio ~1.18, below the 1.25 threshold)
TIME is Daytime (activity started at 15:17, which is in the 09:00-16:59 Daytime window)
Why section references the pace calculation showing elevation-adjusted expected pace
